body:
### Security intent

This control reduces unnecessary exposure by ensuring that **encrypt traffic to HTTPS load balancers with TLS  certificates**.

### Network condition reviewed

The assessment reviews encrypt traffic to HTTPS load balancers with TLS certificates in Kubernetes. It focuses on rules, endpoints, listeners, or policies that make the service reachable in a riskier way than intended.

### Expected posture

Public reachability should be deliberate, documented, and tightly scoped. Administrative and data-plane paths should use private connectivity, strong TLS, explicit source ranges, and provider-native controls rather than broad internet access.
name: Encrypt traffic to HTTPS load balancers with TLS  certificates
remediationDescription:
Your load balancer vendor can provide details on configuring HTTPS with TLS.

**References**

1. [https://kubernetes-sigs.github.io/aws-load-balancer-controller/v2.6/guide/ingress/annotations/#load-balancer-name](https://kubernetes-sigs.github.io/aws-load-balancer-controller/v2.6/guide/ingress/annotations/#load-balancer-name)

2. [https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/create-https-listener.html](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/create-https-listener.html)
description: Encrypt traffic to HTTPS load balancers with TLS certificates
